title: Stress Load Balancing

content.question: Is your stress-to-recovery ratio sustainable?
sop: Rate your current Stress 1-10 across work, health, and personal life combined (see calculation, page 4)
Rate your current Recovery 1-10, sleep, rest, downtime you're actually getting (see calculation, page 4)
Calculate your Balance, Stress divided by Recovery (see calculation, page 4)
If above 1.0, cut one stressor or add one recovery input this week, not both at once.
Re-check weekly, stress load shifts fast and the ratio needs regular re-balancing.
questions: What physical or behavioral sign has been telling you you're over your stress limit that you've been ignoring?
If your stress level stayed exactly where it is today for another 3 months, what would break first?
content.equation_example: Rate Stress 1-10 and Recovery 1-10. Stress 8 ÷ Recovery 3 = 2.67, above 1.0 means stress is outpacing recovery, heading toward burnout. Below 1.0 (Stress 4 ÷ Recovery 8 = 0.5) means recovery keeps pace and the load is sustainable.
content.closing_statement: A sustainable load depends on the ratio, not the total.

caption:
Principle Nº 164: Stress Load Balancing

Too much stress breaks system.

Ask yourself: Is your stress-to-recovery ratio sustainable?
